zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan於1500年出生時，他的父親Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas22歲，母親Catherine St. John29歲。他於1520年與Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater在英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿申維爾索普結婚。他們至少是12個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1531年12月4日在英國英格蘭倫敦享年31歲，埋葬在英國波克夏溫莎女爵聖 格奧智也斯 查阿培爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan于1500年出生时，他的父亲Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas22岁，母亲Catherine St. John29岁。他于1520年与Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater在英国英格兰諾福爾可阿申維爾索普结婚。他们至少是12个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于1531年12月4日在英国英格兰倫敦享年31岁，埋葬在英国波克夏溫莎女爵聖 格奧智也斯 查阿培爾。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Gruffydd
meanings: Welsh: The Welsh name; from creffydd, strong faith, or strong in faith. Gruffydd, a Welsh form of Gryffydd.

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Uryan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and Cornish: variant of   Uren  , occurring mainly in Lancashire. This surname has died out in Britain.
Romanian: from the personal name Urie, a shortened form of Iurie, from Ukrainian Yuriy, an equivalent of Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

variants.name: Uren
meanings: English (of Norman origin, mostly in Cornwall): from the Old French personal name Ev(e)rin, Eurin, either a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Ever- (from the Germanic word for ‘boar’), such as Everard and Everwin (see Erwin ), or an alternative pronunciation of Everwin as /ju:rin/. In Cornwall this name would easily have been confused with the Breton personal name and surname in 2 below.

Cornish (of Breton origin) and Welsh: from the Breton and Welsh personal name Ur(i)en (ultimately from British Celtic ōrbo- perhaps ‘orphan, heir’ + gen- ‘born’). There is evidence for use of the Breton personal name in at least one Anglo-Norman family in Cheshire but in Cornwall it belonged initially, at least, to 16th-century Breton migrants, some of whom also bore it as a surname.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the definite singular form of ur, from Old Norse urth ‘scree, rock-strewn slope’.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan was born in 1500, his father, Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas, was 22 and his mother, Catherine St. John, was 29. He married Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater in 1520, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 12 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 4 December 1531, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 31, and was buried in St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan est né en 1500, son père, Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Catherine St. John, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater en 1520, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 12 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé le 4 décembre 1531, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterré à St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan 1500 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas 22 und seine Mutter Catherine St. John 29. Er hat 1520 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 12 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb am 4. Dezember 1531 im Alter von 31 Jahren in London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan è nato il nel 1500, il suo padre, Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Catherine St. John, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater nel 1520, nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 12 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 4 dicembre 1531, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolto a St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryanが1500年に生まれた時，父Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomasは22歳，母Catherine St. Johnは29歳であった。1520年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペでLady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewaterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも12人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1531年12月4日にイギリスイギリスロンドンで31歳で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソルスト ジョージェ'ス チャペルにて埋葬された。

ko.St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire,: 영국 버크실르 윈드소르 스트 조그'스 차플
ko.St Michael Cornhill, London, England, Un: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 믹하얼 코른힐
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan(이)가 1500년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine St. John(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 애슈웰소에서 Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1531년 12월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 윈드소르 스트 조그'스 차플에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan nasceu em 1500, seu pai, Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Catherine St. John, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater em 1520, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 12 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de dezembro de 1531, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 31 anos, e foi sepultado em St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan родился в 1500 г., его отцу по имени Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Catherine St. John, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater в 1520 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Он умер 4 декабря 1531 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 31 год и был похоронен в местности ст джеорге'с чапел, виндзор, биркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Rhys ap Gruffydd Fitz Uryan nació en 1500, su papá, Gruffydd ap Rhys ap Thomas, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Catherine St. John, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Lady Katherine Howard Duchess of Bridgewater en 1520, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 12 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1531, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultado en St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra.
